Registered Nurse - Surgical

3.936 reviews·View all jobs
Camperdown, Sydney NSW
Nursing - General Medical & Surgical (Healthcare & Medical)
Full time
Add expected salary to your profile for insights
Posted 21d ago
Apply⁠
  • RN Surgical Experience 
  • Must be available to work a 7-day rotating roster 

Chris O’Brien Lifehouse is an internationally recognised independent, not-for-profit cancer hospital leading the way in delivering patient-centred cancer care. We seek passionate, caring, and dedicated staff who align with our Founder’s vision of creating a collaborative environment in which, respect, discovery, empowerment, and nurture, thrive in the interests of our patients. 

About the Role

Experienced RN’s Interested in a specialist surgical career path in any of our specialties, including UGIT, Colorectal, Urology and Gynae.

Essential Skills and Qualifications

  • Working rights in Australia & Current APHRA registration
  • At least 3 years of postgraduate experience
  • Recent Surgical experience – desirable in colorectal, UGIT and urology
  • National Police Check (within the last 6 months or willingness to obtain)
  • Current Working with Children Check (WWCC)
  • Compliance with Category A immunisation requirements

What’s on offer?

  • An attractive salary package with full not-for-profit benefits
  • Fitness passport for you and your family
  • Access EAP

A Workplace That Welcomes Everyone

At Chris O’Brien Lifehouse, we are committed to a culture of inclusion and respect. We value the unique backgrounds and perspectives of our team and actively support, staff from diverse cultural and linguistic backgrounds, LGBTIQA+ communities, Indigenous staff, people living with disabilities, and people of all ages and faiths.

We encourage candidates who meet the selection criteria and shares Chris O'Brien Lifehouse values to apply.

Company profile

3.9·36 reviews
Healthcare Services1,001-5,000 employees

Chris O'Brien Lifehouse (COBL) is a not-for-profit, comprehensive cancer hospital. We treat all types of cancer, specialising in those that are complex and rare. We offer patients everything they need including surgery, chemotherapy, radiation therapy, complementary therapies and supportive services.

Our mission is to minimise the burden of cancer through compassionate, integrated and expert care founded on research, innovation, education and training.

COBL represents a unique model of care and funding in Australia, as a not-for-profit public benevolent institution treating public and private patients. We have no shareholders and income is reinvested for the benefit of our patients.

We offer patients everything they need including surgery, chemotherapy, radiation therapy, complementary therapies and supportive services.

Our mission is to minimise the burden of cancer through compassionate, integrated and expert care founded on research, innovation, education and training.

COBL represents a unique model of care and funding in Australia, as a not-for-profit public benevolent institution treating public and private patients. We have no shareholders and income is reinvested for the benefit of our patients.
